slides.title: Human-In-the-Loop Software Development Agents
key_points: Paper title: Human-In-the-Loop Software Development Agents
Authors: Wannita Takerngsaksiri et al. (Monash, Melbourne, Atlassian)
Year: 2025
Industry-academia collaboration for real-world validation
slides.notes: Establish credibility through Atlassian partnership and highlight industry focus

slides.title: Motivation: Why Human-in-the-Loop Matters
key_points: Existing agents lack human feedback and real-world deployment validation
SWE-bench benchmarks do not reflect complex enterprise contexts
Need to validate if LLM agents actually save time in practice
Industry requires collaborative Human-AI synergy over full automation
slides.notes: Explain WHY this problem is important for real-world software development

slides.title: Problem Definition
key_points: Integrating human feedback into automated software development cycles
Bridging the gap between academic benchmarks and industrial deployment
Evaluating trade-offs between automation speed and code quality
Balancing human oversight with agent autonomy
slides.notes: Define the specific problem this paper addresses

slides.title: Related Work & Positioning
key_points: SWE-agent / AutoCodeRover: Autonomous agents limited to historical benchmarks
Magai / Masai: Multi-agent systems lacking human-in-the-loop mechanisms
Gap: No prior work deployed human-in-the-loop agents in large enterprise
This work: First industrial deployment with human collaboration
slides.notes: Compare approaches and highlight limitations of previous work

slides.title: Core Idea: HULA Framework
key_points: HULA: Human-in-the-Loop Agent framework for JIRA issue resolution
AI Planner and Coding agents collaborate with human engineers
Cooperative paradigm: agents adjust based on human feedback
Deployed directly into production Atlassian JIRA instance
slides.notes: Present the main contribution in ONE clear concept

slides.title: Method Overview: Multi-Agent Architecture
key_points: Three agent types: AI Planner, AI Coding Agent, Human Agent
Four-stage workflow: Setup, Planning, Coding, Raising PR
Cooperative DPDE paradigm for iterative refinement
Direct integration with Atlassian's internal JIRA instance

slides.title: Method Details: Agent Roles & Interaction
key_points: AI Planner Agent: Localizes files and generates coding plans
AI Coding Agent: Generates code with self-refinement via linter feedback
Human Agent: Reviews plans, modifies code, provides corrective guidance
Shared memory architecture for context access without direct communication
slides.notes: Technical details of each agent's responsibilities

slides.title: Experiment Setup
key_points: Datasets: SWE-bench Verified (500 issues), Atlassian Internal (369 issues)
Baseline: SWE-agent Claude (Leaderboard comparison)
Metrics: Plan/Code Generation Rate, Approval Rate, PR Merge Rate
Environment: Production Atlassian JIRA with 2,600+ practitioners
slides.notes: Establish experimental rigor and real-world deployment scale

slides.title: Results: Key Findings
key_points: 82% plan approval rate indicates high trust in AI planning
59% merged PR rate for raised pull requests shows practical utility
8% of total issues resulted in successfully merged HULA-assisted code
Performance gap: 86% recall on SWE-bench vs 30% internally

slides.title: Limitations & Critical Analysis
key_points: Significant performance drop on internal data vs open-source benchmarks
Generated code often functionally incorrect without human edits
High cognitive load: users must write detailed issue descriptions
GPT-4 dependency limits reproducibility and raises cost concerns
slides.notes: Honest assessment of weaknesses and deployment challenges

slides.title: Takeaways & Discussion Questions
key_points: Human-in-the-loop is critical for enterprise-grade software generation
Detailed issue descriptions are vital for agent performance
Q1: How to reduce cognitive load of writing detailed prompts?
Q2: Does human verification negate productivity gains of automation?
slides.notes: Summarize insights and prompt group discussion
